highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.152 Released
highlights.summary: The `/code-review --fix` command now automatically applies review suggestions directly to your working tree, making it easier to act on reuse, simplification, and efficiency improvements. The `/simplify` command now delegates to `/code-review --fix` under the hood. Skills and slash commands can also restrict which tools are available via a new `disallowed-tools` frontmatter option.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.149 Released
highlights.summary: The `/usage` command now breaks down your usage limits by category — skills, subagents, plugins, and individual MCP servers — so you can see exactly what is consuming your quota. The `/diff` detail view gained full keyboard navigation support, and Markdown output rendering has been improved.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.147 Released
highlights.summary: Pinned background sessions (created with `Ctrl+T` in `claude agents`) now stay alive when idle and survive Claude Code updates by restarting in place. The `/simplify` command has been renamed to `/code-review`, which now also surfaces correctness bugs in addition to style improvements.

highlights.title: Introducing Claude Opus 4.7
highlights.summary: Anthropic has announced Claude Opus 4.7, the latest addition to the Opus model family. This new model brings enhanced capabilities that can be leveraged directly within Claude Code for more powerful coding assistance and complex task handling.

highlights.title: Claude Code as a Daily Driver: Claude.md, Skills, Subagents, Plugins, and MCPs
highlights.summary: This in-depth guide covers how to use Claude Code as your primary development tool, walking through key concepts like Claude.md configuration, custom skills, subagents, plugins, and MCP integrations. It is a practical resource for developers who want to move beyond basic usage and build a productive daily workflow.

highlights.title: Show HN: Spec-Driven Development Workflow for Claude Code
highlights.summary: This community-built workflow proposes a structured approach to using coding agents by first generating detailed specs (requirements, code analysis, design) before splitting implementation into subtasks. The decomposition strategy helps Claude Code produce more accurate and maintainable results on complex projects.

highlights.title: Complete Claude Code Course In 2 Hours For Developers
highlights.summary: This comprehensive two-hour YouTube course walks developers through Claude Code from setup to advanced usage, making it a solid resource for anyone who prefers video learning. It covers the core features developers need to become productive with Claude Code in VS Code.

highlights.title: Mastering Claude Code in 30 minutes
highlights.summary: A focused 30-minute video tutorial designed to get developers up to speed with Claude Code quickly, covering the most important features without overwhelming beginners. It is a great starting point if you want a concise overview before diving into longer courses.

tips_of_the_day: Run `/code-review --fix` after finishing a feature to let Claude automatically clean up inefficiencies and simplify code — it now applies changes directly to your working tree so you don't have to do it manually.
Check `/usage` regularly to see which skills, subagents, or MCP servers are consuming the most quota; identifying expensive operations early helps you optimize your workflow before hitting limits.

When tackling a large feature with Claude Code, write a brief spec file first (requirements, design decisions, subtask breakdown) and reference it in your prompt — this spec-driven approach dramatically improves accuracy and reduces back-and-forth.
